007e7d6cb64a7dfcdcad9fc6f4f0ba69fdc88203: Existing LLMs heavily rely on cloud computing, leading to long response times, high bandwidth costs, and significant privacy concerns, especially for real-time and sensitive applications.

Edge computing is a promising solution but faces challenges due to LLMs' computation-intensive and resource-greedy nature (e.g., Llama2-7B requires 28GB memory, exceeding most edge device capacities).
Collaborative Edge Computing (CEC) is proposed to address these issues by integrating resources from geo-distributed edge devices and cloud servers, enabling efficient LLM inference.

EdgeShard is a novel framework for collaborative LLM inference on heterogeneous edge devices and cloud servers, which partitions LLMs into shards and allocates them to devices to optimize latency and throughput.

Experimental results show EdgeShard significantly outperforms baseline methods, reducing latency by up to 50% and achieving 2x throughput compared to on-device and vertical cloud-edge collaborative inference.
929305892d4ddae575a0fc23227a8139f7681632: State-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-4, Claude v1.3) remain vulnerable to jailbreak attacks despite extensive red-teaming and safety training efforts.
Two primary failure modes, "competing objectives" and "mismatched generalization," explain why safety training fails and guide the design of new, effective attacks.
New attacks leveraging these failure modes significantly outperform existing ad hoc jailbreaks, succeeding on 100% of curated red-teaming prompts and over 96% of evaluated prompts.

Jailbreaks are inherent to current safety training methods, suggesting that scaling alone will not resolve these vulnerabilities; safety mechanisms require parity with the underlying model's capabilities.
